hero.subheading: A step-by-step interactive guide for Indian voters — check eligibility, register on NVSP, and prepare for polling day.
hero.ctaSubtext: Takes less than 2 minutes · No account needed
hero.searchPlaceholder: Ask anything — eligibility, dates, how to vote…
hero.tooltipData: Data sourced from official NVSP/ECI guidelines

overview.title: The election process, step by step
overview.subtitle: Elections follow a clear process. Here's the big picture so nothing surprises you.

items.title: Election Schedule Announced
items.body: The Election Commission of India (ECI) announces the election schedule. The Model Code of Conduct comes into effect immediately.

items.title: Voter Registration (Form 6)
items.body: New voters register using Form 6 on nvsp.in or the Voter Helpline App. Existing voters update details using Form 8.

items.title: Nomination & Campaigning
items.body: Candidates file nominations. Parties campaign across constituencies. Campaigning stops 48 hours before polling day.

items.title: Polling Day
items.body: Registered voters visit their assigned polling booth and vote using the EVM. Booths open 7 AM to 6 PM.

items.title: Vote Counting (EVM)
items.body: EVMs are counted at designated centres under strict ECI supervision.

items.title: Results & Government Formation
items.body: Results are declared by the Returning Officer. The winning party forms the government after swearing-in.

timeline.title: Important deadlines to know
timeline.subtitle: Click any stage to expand details. The highlighted step is where you are today.
timeline.callout: Dates shown are sample data. Always verify with your official local election authority.

eligibility.title: Check your eligibility
eligibility.subtitle: Fill in the form — results update as you type.
eligibility.req1: Must be 18 or older (on Jan 1 of qualifying year)
eligibility.req3: Must be registered on the electoral roll
eligibility.ageAway: You must be 18+ to vote. You're {{years}} years away.
eligibility.ageAlmost: Almost there — you need to be {{years}} more year{{s}} older.
eligibility.previewForeign: Foreign nationals cannot vote in Indian elections.
eligibility.previewNRI: NRIs can vote in person at their registered constituency.
eligibility.previewTooYoung: You need to be {{years}} more year{{s}} older to vote.
eligibility.previewLookingGood: Looking good! Complete the form to confirm.

guide.title: Step-by-step voting guide
guide.subtitle: Click each step to expand. Check off steps as you complete them.

steps.title: Register on NVSP / Voter App
steps.subtitle: Get your name on the electoral roll
steps.body: Every Indian citizen aged 18+ must be registered on the electoral roll to vote. Register online at nvsp.in or via the Voter Helpline App. It takes under 5 minutes.

items.title: Visit nvsp.in or download Voter Helpline App
items.desc: Available on Android and iOS. You can also register at your local BLO office.

items.title: Fill Form 6 (new registration)
items.desc: Provide your name, DOB, address, and upload a passport-size photo and address proof.

items.title: Check your name on the roll
items.desc: Visit electoralsearch.eci.gov.in and search by name, EPIC number, or mobile number.

items.title: Receive your EPIC card
items.desc: Your Voter ID (EPIC) card will be dispatched to your registered address within 30 days.

steps.title: Verify Your Documents
steps.subtitle: Know what to carry on polling day
steps.body: You must carry at least one of the 12 approved photo IDs to vote. Your EPIC card is the primary ID, but Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, and others are also accepted.

items.title: EPIC Card (Voter ID)
items.desc: Your primary voter identity card issued by ECI. Carry the original — photocopies are not accepted.

items.title: e-EPIC on mobile
items.desc: Download your digital Voter ID from the Voter Helpline App or nvsp.in — accepted at booths.

items.title: Check your booth address
items.desc: Your polling booth is printed on your EPIC card. Also check on the Voter Helpline App.

items.title: Aadhaar as backup
items.desc: If you do not have your EPIC, Aadhaar card is accepted as an alternative photo ID.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote (EVM)
steps.subtitle: What happens inside the polling booth
steps.body: India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs). Voting is simple, fast, and completely secret.

items.title: Find your polling booth
items.desc: Check your EPIC card or the Voter Helpline App. Booths are open 7 AM to 6 PM on polling day.

items.title: Show your ID and get marked
items.desc: Show your EPIC or approved alternative ID. The officer will mark your finger with indelible ink.

items.title: Press the EVM button
items.desc: Enter the voting compartment. Press the blue button next to your chosen candidate on the EVM.

items.title: Verify on VVPAT
items.desc: A paper slip appears in the VVPAT machine for 7 seconds — verify your vote, then it drops into the sealed box.

documents.title: What you'll need
documents.subtitle: Requirements vary by region. Confirm with your local authority.

items.name: EPIC Card (Voter ID)
items.purpose: Primary photo ID issued by ECI — most widely accepted at booths

items.name: Aadhaar Card
items.purpose: Accepted as alternative photo ID at polling booths

items.name: MNREGA Job Card
items.purpose: Accepted for rural voters as alternative ID

items.name: Smart Card (CGHS / ECHS)
items.purpose: Health smart cards accepted as alternative ID

items.name: Pension Document (with photo)
items.purpose: Accepted for senior citizens as alternative ID

items.name: Disability Certificate (with photo)
items.purpose: Accepted for PwD voters as alternative ID

faq.title: Common questions answered
faq.subtitle: Search below or browse by category. Can't find what you need? Ask the assistant.
faq.noResultsSub: Try a different keyword, or ask the assistant directly.
faq.stillHaveQuestionsSub: Our assistant can answer anything about voting, registration, and election day.

items.q: Who is eligible to vote in India?
items.a: Any Indian citizen aged 18 or above on January 1 of the qualifying year who is ordinarily resident in a constituency is eligible. You must be registered on the electoral roll.

items.q: How do I register as a new voter?
items.a: Visit nvsp.in or download the Voter Helpline App. Fill Form 6 with your name, date of birth, address, and a passport-size photo. You can also visit your local BLO (Booth Level Officer) office.
EPIC stands for Electors' Photo Identity Card — commonly called the Voter ID card. It is issued by the Election Commission of India and is the primary identity document for voting.

items.q: I do not have my EPIC card. Can I still vote?
items.a: Yes. ECI accepts 12 alternative photo IDs including Aadhaar, Passport, Driving Licence, PAN card, MNREGA Job Card, Bank Passbook with photo, and more. You can also use the e-EPIC (digital Voter ID) on your phone.

items.q: When is the last date to register?
items.a: Registration closes approximately 30 days before the election. Check the Key Dates section for the exact deadline for the current election cycle.

items.q: What is an EVM and is it reliable?
items.a: An Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) is a standalone device used to record votes. It is not connected to the internet. The VVPAT machine lets you verify your vote on a paper slip for 7 seconds.
NOTA stands for "None of the Above." It is the last option on every EVM ballot. If you are not satisfied with any candidate, you can press NOTA. Your vote is counted but does not go to any candidate.

items.q: Can I vote if I am away from my constituency?
items.a: You must vote in your registered constituency. However, senior citizens (80+), persons with disabilities (PwD), and essential service workers can apply for a Postal Ballot.

items.q: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
items.a: The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) is a set of guidelines issued by ECI that governs the conduct of political parties and candidates during elections. It comes into effect when the election schedule is announced.

items.q: How do I report election violations?
items.a: Use the cVIGIL app to report MCC violations with photo or video evidence. ECI guarantees a response within 100 minutes. You can also call the National Voter Helpline at 1950.

items.q: Can my employer stop me from voting?
items.a: No. Under Section 135B of the Representation of the People Act, every employer must give employees paid leave on polling day. Refusing to do so is a punishable offence.

items.q: What is the Voter Helpline number?
items.a: The National Voter Helpline number is 1950. You can call to check your voter registration, find your polling booth, report issues, or get any election-related information.

flow.introMsg:
🙏 Namaste! I'm VoteGenie, your personal India election assistant.

I'll help you check if you can vote in the 2026 elections. Let's start with a few quick questions.

How old are you?
flow.citizenYes: 🇮🇳 Yes, I am an Indian citizen
flow.citizenForeign: ✈️ I am a foreign national / OCI
flow.stateQuestion: Which state or Union Territory are you from?
flow.ageOk:
Great! {{age}} years old — you meet the age requirement ✓

Are you an Indian citizen?
flow.ageTooYoung:
You're {{age}} years old. The minimum voting age in India is 18.

Are you an Indian citizen?
flow.citizenPerfect:
Perfect! Indian citizens can vote in national and state elections.

Which state or UT are you from?
flow.stateGotIt:
Got it{{state}}!

Are you currently registered on the electoral roll?

resultForeign.headline: Not Eligible ❌
resultForeign.reason: Foreign nationals and OCI card holders cannot vote in Indian elections. Only Indian citizens are eligible.

resultNRI.headline: NRI — Special Rules Apply ⚠️
resultNRI.reason: NRIs holding an Indian passport can register as overseas voters and vote in person at their registered constituency. Register using Form 6A on nvsp.in.

resultTooYoung.headline: Not Yet Eligible ❌
resultTooYoung.reason: You must be 18 years old on January 1 of the qualifying year. You are {{years}} year{{s}} away from your first vote. Save this app — it'll be ready when you are! 🗳️

resultNotRegistered.headline: Eligible — But Not Registered 📝
resultNotRegistered.reason: Great news! You are eligible to vote{{state}}. But you need to register first. It takes under 5 minutes on nvsp.in.

resultUnsure.headline: Check Your Registration 🔍
resultUnsure.reason: You may be eligible to vote{{state}}. Check your name on the electoral roll first.

resultEligible.headline: You're Eligible to Vote! 🎉
resultEligible.reason: Congratulations! You are eligible to vote{{state}}. Here's what to do next:

flow.suggPostal: 📮 Postal ballot available in your state

chat.title: VoteGenie
suggestions: How do I register on NVSP?
chat.cleared: Chat cleared! Ask me anything about voting in India. 🗳️

kb.greeting:
🙏 Namaste! I'm VoteGenie, your India election assistant.

Ask me anything about:
• Eligibility to vote
• How to register on NVSP
• What to carry to the booth
• EVM & VVPAT
• NOTA
• Voter Helpline 1950
kb.init:
🙏 Namaste! I'm VoteGenie.

Ask me anything about voting in India — eligibility, NVSP registration, EPIC card, EVM, NOTA, or polling day process.
kb.eligible:
To vote in India you need to:
• Be **18 or older** on January 1 of the qualifying year
• Be an **Indian citizen**
• Be **registered** on the electoral roll

Use the **Eligibility Checker** on this page for a personalised result, or click **Check if You Can Vote** above.
kb.register:
To register as a new voter:
1. Visit **nvsp.in** or download the Voter Helpline App
2. Fill **Form 6** with your name, DOB, address, and photo
3. Submit — you'll get a reference number
4. Your **EPIC card** arrives within 30 days

You can also visit your local **BLO (Booth Level Officer)** office.
kb.epic:
**EPIC** stands for Electors' Photo Identity Card — your Voter ID.

If you don't have it, ECI accepts **12 alternative IDs** including:
• Aadhaar card
• Passport
• Driving licence
• PAN card
• MNREGA Job Card

You can also use **e-EPIC** (digital Voter ID) on your phone from nvsp.in.
kb.aadhaar:
Yes! **Aadhaar card** is accepted as an alternative photo ID at polling booths.

If you don't have your EPIC card on polling day, Aadhaar works as a valid substitute.
kb.evm:
**EVM (Electronic Voting Machine)** is used in all Indian elections.

• It is a **standalone device** — not connected to the internet
• **VVPAT** (Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail) lets you verify your vote on a paper slip for **7 seconds**
• EVMs are tested and sealed before elections under ECI supervision
kb.vvpat:
After pressing the EVM button, a **paper slip** appears in the VVPAT machine for **7 seconds** showing:
• The candidate's name
• Party symbol
• Serial number

The slip then drops into a sealed box. This lets you verify your vote was recorded correctly.
kb.nota:
**NOTA** = None of the Above.

It's the **last option** on every EVM ballot. If you're not satisfied with any candidate, press NOTA.

Your vote is counted but doesn't go to any candidate. It's your right to reject all candidates.
kb.deadline:
Registration closes approximately **30 days before the election**.

Check the **Key Dates** section on this page for the exact deadline for the 2026 election cycle.

Some states also have special windows — check with your local election office.
kb.documents:
Carry to the polling booth:
• 🪪 **EPIC card** (primary ID)
• OR any of 12 alternatives (Aadhaar, Passport, PAN, etc.)
• 📱 **e-EPIC** on your phone is also accepted

Check the **Documents** section on this page for the full list.
kb.postal:
You must vote in your **registered constituency**.

However, **Postal Ballot** is available for:
• Senior citizens (80+)
• Persons with disabilities (PwD)
• Essential service workers

Apply for postal ballot before the deadline through your Returning Officer.
kb.mcc:
The **Model Code of Conduct (MCC)** is a set of ECI guidelines that comes into effect when the election schedule is announced.

It governs:
• Political party behaviour
• Government announcements
• Use of government resources

Violations can be reported via the **cVIGIL app** — ECI responds within 100 minutes.
kb.cvigil:
Use the **cVIGIL app** to report election violations:
• Take a photo or video of the violation
• Submit through the app
• ECI guarantees a response within **100 minutes**

You can also call the **National Voter Helpline: 1950**
kb.helpline:
📞 **National Voter Helpline: 1950**

Call to:
• Check your voter registration
• Find your polling booth
• Report issues
• Get election information

Available in multiple languages.
kb.secret:
Yes — your vote is **completely secret** in India. 🔒

The EVM records your vote without linking it to your identity.

Also: Under **Section 135B** of the Representation of the People Act, your employer **must give you paid leave** on polling day. Refusing is a punishable offence.
kb.nri:
**NRIs** holding an Indian passport can vote!

Register as an overseas voter using **Form 6A** on nvsp.in.

You must vote **in person** at your registered constituency in India — postal/proxy voting is not available for NRIs currently.
kb.electoralSearch:
Check your name on the electoral roll at:
🔗 **electoralsearch.eci.gov.in**

Search by:
• Name + state
• EPIC number
• Mobile number

If your name is missing, contact your local **BLO (Booth Level Officer)** immediately.
kb.booth:
Your polling booth is assigned based on your registered address.

Find it:
• On your **EPIC card**
• On the **Voter Helpline App**
• At **electoralsearch.eci.gov.in**

Polling booths are open **7 AM – 6 PM** on polling day.
kb.howToVote:
On polling day:
1. Carry your EPIC card or approved alternative ID
2. Go to your assigned polling booth
3. Show ID — officer marks your finger with **indelible ink**
4. Press the **blue EVM button** next to your candidate
5. Verify on **VVPAT** (7 seconds)

See the **Voting Guide** section for full details.
kb.thanks:
You're welcome! 😊 Every vote counts — make yours count in 2026! 🗳️

Anything else you'd like to know?
kb.fallback:
I'm not sure about that. Try asking about:
• Eligibility to vote
• How to register (NVSP)
• EPIC / Voter ID card
• EVM and VVPAT
• NOTA
• Voter Helpline 1950
• Polling booth location

footer.title: 🗳️ ElectionGuide
footer.description: A civic education resource for first-time voters.
footer.disclaimer: All information is for educational purposes only. Always verify with your official local election authority.
